svg.text: Operational Flow And Exceptions
Typical Work Flow
1. A member joins in ClubSpark.
2. ClubSpark sends a notification email and updates its member/contact records.
3. n8n captures the email and refreshes the latest ClubSpark exports.
4. The local database combines those sources into a clean signup record.
5. A batch is created for new items that need to be fulfilled.
6. The batch produces labels, envelope PDFs, and exception lists.
7. Where an address is missing, the member can be contacted by email instead of post.
8. Once the physical admin work is done, the batch is marked complete.
9. Reporting is synced to Metabase so the committee can monitor progress.
Exception Handling
• Missing signup capture: if ClubSpark shows a valid membership but no signup email was captured,
the workflow can create the missing record.
• No-address handling: if there is no usable postal address, the system can send an email instead
of producing post.
• Manual batch items: extra shoe tags, parent tags, or keys can be added without pretending there
was a new signup.
• History snapshots: periodic snapshots preserve membership counts for trend reporting over time.
Summary
At a committee level, the system can be thought of as a membership operations pipeline:
• ClubSpark provides the raw membership events and data
• n8n turns that into usable admin work
• the local database tracks what has happened and what still needs doing
• print files, emails, and dashboards are the outputs the committee sees
The main benefit is that signups, address handling, batch preparation, and reporting are kept in one
joined-up process instead of being managed manually across separate spreadsheets and inboxes.
